# Bug report

Thanks for taking the time to file a bug report. The clearer the report, the
faster the fix.

## Environment

- Windows version and build (`winver`):
- PowerShell version (`$PSVersionTable.PSVersion`):
- PatchCleaner Revisited commit or release tag:
- Are you running Windows PowerShell 5.1 or PowerShell 7?

## What happened

A clear and concise description of the bug.

## Reproduction steps

1. Run `.\patchCleanerRevisited.ps1` as Administrator
2. Click …
3. See error …

## Expected behaviour

What you expected to happen.

## Actual behaviour

What actually happened. Include the full error message and stack trace.

## Screenshots

If applicable, attach a screenshot of the dialog or window. **Do not include
sensitive information** (usernames, license keys, product codes from your
machine, full registry paths you do not want to share).

## Severity

- [ ] Critical - script crashes on launch or corrupts the Installer database
- [ ] High - destructive action completes but produces wrong result
- [ ] Medium - feature behaves incorrectly but no data loss
- [ ] Low - cosmetic, documentation, or minor UX issue

## Checklist

- [ ] I have read the [Security and Backup Disclaimer](../README.md#security-and-backup-disclaimer)
- [ ] I have a full system backup
- [ ] I have a system restore point
- [ ] I reproduced the issue on a non-production machine if possible
